data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260514T200804-4c03265c/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260514T200804-4c03265c/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260514T200804-4c03265c/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260514T200804-4c03265c/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260514T200804-4c03265c/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-71629938">
    <title>execute-loop: thread execute-loop-failed-routes into Fizeau RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
DDx records execute-loop-failed-routes on every provider_connectivity failure but never passes the list to Fizeau on the next attempt. Fizeau therefore re-selects the same (provider, model) and the bead loops on the same dead route until the operator intervenes. Observed in the wild: with bragi unreachable, ddx-b4edab35 first failed at 15:48 routing to bragi/qwen3.5-27b. After the 15-min cooldown the second attempt at 16:45 again routed to bragi/qwen3.5-27b and failed identically. The bead's failed-routes list grew but had no operational effect.

ROOT CAUSE
cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go around the RouteRequest construction does not populate any caller-side exclusion field. The bead's execute-loop-failed-routes Extra is read only by recentProviderConnectivityMinPower (cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:409) to nudge MinPower, never to express "do not pick this (provider, model)". This depends on Fizeau exposing the exclusion field (filed upstream as fizeau-8134d6db).

PROPOSED FIX
- After Fizeau bead fizeau-8134d6db lands and the new RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes is available in the Fizeau release we depend on, read the bead's execute-loop-failed-routes list before each route request and populate ExcludedRoutes with the (provider, model) tuples whose At timestamp is within the configured exclusion window (default 1h; configurable).
- Add an exclusion-expiry sweep: drop entries older than the window from the in-request list (the bead still keeps the history for audit, but stale exclusions do not block recovery once the provider returns).
- When ExcludedRoutes leaves no viable candidate at the requested power class, escalate the bead's TriagePowerHintKey (composing with ddx-8a7a6843's ladder-exhaustion changes).

NON-SCOPE
- Do not garbage-collect the bead's failed-routes audit list; only filter in-request.
- Do not implement Fizeau-side exclusion (covered by fizeau-8134d6db).
- Do not change cooldown duration.

DEPS
fizeau-8134d6db, ddx-8a7a6843.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. TestRouteRequest_PopulatedFromFailedRoutes asserts execute-loop-failed-routes within the exclusion window appear in the Fizeau RouteRequest.ExcludedRoutes payload.
2. TestRouteRequest_ExpiredFailedRoutesDropped asserts entries older than the exclusion window are omitted from the RouteRequest but remain in the bead Extra audit list.
3. TestRouteRequest_AllRoutesExcludedTriggersEscalation asserts that when every candidate at the requested power class is excluded, TriagePowerHintKey escalates per the ddx-8a7a6843 path.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... green.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:routing, area:execution, kind:integration, phase:2, spec:endpoint-first-routing</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="1bb8e103b6f4484ba944f079b30e1e2726d20178" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260514T200804-4c03265c"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
All pre-commit checks pass and the commit succeeded. 

**Summary of changes**:

- **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`**: Added `RouteExclusionWindow` (1h constant), `buildExcludedRoutes` (converts `FailedRouteEntry` list to Fizeau `ExcludedRoute` values filtered by time window), and `CheckAndApplyRouteExclusions` (pre-dispatch check that calls `ResolveRoute` with the exclusions and escalates `TriagePowerHintKey` when no viable candidate remains).

- **`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_route_exclusion_test.go`**: Added the 3 required tests: `TestRouteRequest_PopulatedFromFailedRoutes`, `TestRouteRequest_ExpiredFailedRoutesDropped`, `TestRouteRequest_AllRoutesExcludedTriggersEscalation`.

- **`cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go`**: Wired `CheckAndApplyRouteExclusions` into the executor closure for free-routing attempts — calls `ResolveRoute` with the bead's recent failed routes as `ExcludedRoutes`, returns early with escalated `TriagePowerHintKey` if no viable candidate exists.
